verified.notes: Home Delivered Meals in Washington is administered through multiple regional providers (O3A in coastal counties, Sound Generations in King County, etc.) with varying eligibility criteria. Age 60+ is the standard senior threshold across programs; O3A also serves 55+ American Indian/Alaska Native seniors. Income limits exist (O3A: 185% FPL) but are not consistently documented across all providers in search results. No asset limits found in authoritative sources. Phone number is O3A Senior Nutrition Coordinator for Clallam, Jefferson, Grays Harbor, Pacific Counties. For statewide information, WA 211 (https://search.wa211.org/) aggregates 76 providers but does not list centralized phone. Sound Generations (King County) accepts applications online; Mom's Meals requires contacting case manager/health plan. No single unified state program phone number identified.

verified.notes: Age requirement (55+) confirmed from federal DOL source. Income limit of 125% of federal poverty level is documented but specific dollar amounts vary annually by family size and are not provided in search results. No asset limits found in search results. Phone number for Washington state SCSEP not found in search results; Goodwill WA listing shows program currently on hold. AARP Foundation operates SCSEP in Washington with phone (206) 624-6698 but this is a nonprofit grantee, not official state agency line. Recommend contacting Washington Department of Employment Security or DOL directly for current Washington-specific phone number and income thresholds.

programs.programName: Housing and Essential Needs (HEN) Referral Program

verified.notes: HEN program does not have a senior-specific age threshold (55+). Program eligibility is based on temporary incapacity/disability and inability to work for 90+ days, not age. Minimum age requirement is 18 (general population). No senior pathway exists. Income and asset limits not specified in available sources. Program appears active as of 2026.

programs.programName: Aged, Blind, or Disabled (ABD) Cash Assistance Program

verified.notes: Age 65+ is the senior-specific threshold; program also serves blind individuals and disabled adults of any age. Asset limit of $12,000 updated from $6,000 in 2024. No income test found in search results. Official phone number not available in provided sources. Search results do not include .gov URLs; most authoritative source is Washington Department of Social and Health Services via Palouse Resources (dated 05/02/2025) and homeless services training materials (dated 09/10/2025).
